skill.name: internal-comms-community
skill.description: A set of resources to help write all kinds of internal communications using company-specific formats
skill.summary: Internal communications writing assistant with templates for 3P updates, newsletters, reports, and FAQs
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 15 potential security patterns, all confirmed as false positives upon manual review. LICENSE.txt contains standard Apache 2.0 license text with legitimate URLs. SKILL.md references example file paths (e.g., examples/3p-updates.md) that were misidentified as shell commands. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code, no network operations, and no security risks. Safe to publish.

content.user_title: Write Internal Communications with Company Templates
content.value_statement: Struggle to write internal updates in your company format? This skill provides ready-to-use templates for 3P updates, newsletters, status reports, and FAQs so you can communicate clearly and consistently.
actual_capabilities: Generate 3P (Progress, Plans, Problems) team updates following structured formats
Create company-wide newsletters with proper tone and organization
Write FAQ responses with clear explanations
Draft status reports for leadership and stakeholders
Compose incident reports with technical accuracy
Format project updates with consistent structure
limitations: Requires user to provide specific content about their situation
Templates may need customization for unique company cultures
Cannot access real company data or internal systems
User must verify accuracy before sending communications

use_cases.title: Weekly Team Status Reports
use_cases.description: Engineering leads write 3P updates to keep leadership aligned on team progress, upcoming plans, and blocking issues

use_cases.title: Company-Wide Announcements
use_cases.description: HR and communications teams draft newsletters and leadership updates to share company news with all employees
use_cases.target_user: HR professionals and internal comms teams

use_cases.title: Incident Response Documentation
use_cases.description: Site reliability engineers write structured incident reports to communicate what happened during outages

prompt_templates.title: Write a 3P Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a 3P update for my team this week. Progress: We launched the new authentication system. Plans: Next week we will add rate limiting. Problems: We're blocked on database migration approvals.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Company Newsletter
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a company newsletter announcing our new product launch, two customer success stories, and upcoming team building events next month

prompt_templates.title: Answer Employee FAQ
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an FAQ response explaining the new remote work policy: employees can work from home 3 days per week, must be in office on Tuesdays and Thursdays for team collaboration

prompt_templates.title: Create Incident Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an incident report for the 2-hour outage yesterday. Root cause was database connection pool exhaustion. Fix: Increased pool size. Prevention: Added monitoring alerts.

output_examples.input: Write a 3P update. Progress: Deployed login feature. Plans: Add password reset. Problems: API rate limits blocking testing.
output_examples.output:
**3P Update - Week of Feb 24**

**Progress**
- Successfully deployed the new login feature to production
- Completed integration testing with auth provider
- Reduced login latency by 40%

**Plans**
- Implement password reset functionality
- Add two-factor authentication option
- Conduct security audit before release

**Problems**
- API rate limits blocking automated testing
- Need approval for increased API quota
- Workaround: Testing in batches manually

output_examples.input: Create a company newsletter about product launch
output_examples.output:
**Company Newsletter - February 2026**

**Big News: Product Launch!** 🚀

We're excited to announce that our new product is now live. After months of hard work from the entire team, customers can now experience the faster, more intuitive interface.

**What's Next**
- Training sessions: March 5-7
- Customer webinars: Sign up in the portal
- Feedback channels: Use the product-feedback Slack channel

**Thank You**
Huge thanks to everyone who contributed to making this launch possible. Your dedication made the difference!

output_examples.input: Write an FAQ response about the new PTO policy
output_examples.output:
**FAQ: New PTO Policy Explained**

**Q: How many PTO days do I get?**
A: Full-time employees now receive 20 days of paid time off annually, plus 5 sick days and 10 company holidays.

**Q: When does this take effect?**
A: The new policy is effective March 1, 2026.

**Q: What happens to my accrued PTO?**
A: All previously accrued PTO will be carried over. You can use it according to the existing rollover rules.

**Q: Do I need manager approval for PTO?**
A: Yes, please request PTO through HRIS at least 2 weeks in advance for requests longer than 3 days.

**Q: Who can I contact with questions?**
A: Reach out to HR@company.com or book a time with HR during open office hours (Tuesdays 2-4 PM).

best_practices: Provide specific details about your situation rather than vague requests
Review the generated communication against your company's actual guidelines before sending
Use this skill as a starting point and customize the tone for your organization
Include relevant data and metrics in progress reports for maximum impact
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to write sensitive HR actions like terminations or disciplinary notices
Do not share confidential company information or trade secrets in prompts
Do not rely solely on AI-generated content without human review for important communications
Do not use this skill for external customer communications or marketing materials

faq.question: What communication types can this skill help write?
faq.answer: This skill supports 3P updates (Progress, Plans, Problems), company newsletters, FAQ responses, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.

faq.question: Do I need to provide my company's communication guidelines?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. The skill includes common formatting templates. For best results, describe any specific requirements your company has.

faq.question: Can this skill access my company's previous communications?
faq.answer: No. This skill cannot access your company data or past communications. You need to provide relevant context in your prompt.

faq.question: How do I get the best results from this skill?
faq.answer: Be specific about your situation. Include concrete details, metrics, and context rather than general requests.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates for my company?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the generated output as a starting point and modify it to match your company's tone and format preferences.

faq.question: Is this skill appropriate for sensitive communications?
faq.answer: Use your judgment. This skill works well for routine updates and documentation. For sensitive matters like layoffs, legal issues, or crisis communications, work directly with HR and legal teams.
